location.office_time_required: 4 days a week in one of our SF Bay Area offices

level_signals: 8-10+ years of experience in technical program management, infrastructure, or SRE
at least 3-5 years managing infra or platform-scale programs
Comp range $198K-$235.5K base consistent with Senior IC at a late-stage startup

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: BS/MS in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.
8-10+ years of experience in technical program management, infrastructure, or SRE, with at least 3-5 years managing infra or platform-scale programs.
at least 3-5 years managing infra or platform-scale programs.
Proven success delivering cross-functional infrastructure programs in B2B or enterprise environments where scalability, uptime, and performance are critical.
Experience working closely with Infra, SRE, and ML/AI teams on distributed systems or data infrastructure.
Strong understanding of cloud infrastructure (AWS, GCP, or Azure) including compute, networking, storage, and orchestration systems.
Ability to structure complex multi-quarter infrastructure programs with clear milestones and measurable impact.
Strong written and verbal communication and ability to manage through ambiguity, anticipate scaling challenges, and align teams across priorities.
Builder mindset with focus on automation, reliability, and efficiency.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Understanding of data pipelines, ML training workflows, and LLM runtime infrastructure is a plus.

scope.surface: Infrastructure platform — Setup & Deployment, Runtime, Storage, AI Infra, ML pipelines, LLM serving stack
core_responsibilities: Driving the company's Infrastructure roadmap across Setup & Deployment, Runtime, Storage, and AI Infra
Leading cross-functional programs that improve scalability, reliability, cost efficiency, and developer velocity
Defining and orchestrating how Glean instances are deployed, upgraded, and monitored at scale
Partnering with AI and Data teams to evolve ML pipelines, model training infrastructure, and LLM serving stack
Leading initiatives to improve observability, configuration management, and resource utilization
Coordinating capacity planning, infrastructure migrations, and performance optimization programs
Building clear visibility into infra cost drivers and partnering with finance and engineering leaders on optimization initiatives
Lead end-to-end infra programs spanning compute, networking, storage, orchestration, and AI workloads
Develop and operationalize frameworks for runtime health, scaling, and disaster recovery
Communicate program status and technical risks effectively to leadership and stakeholders

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as potentially eligible alongside variable compensation

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($198K-$235.5K base)
Clear scope and ownership area (infra platform across compute, storage, orchestration, AI infra)
Role sits at intersection of infrastructure and AI/ML — high-impact growth area
Well-known, late-stage AI startup with strong press recognition (Fast Company Top 10, Forbes AI 50, etc.)
Comprehensive benefits package described including 401k, education and wellness stipends, home office stipend
red_flags: Requires 4 days/week in SF Bay Area offices — very high in-office time, eliminates most remote candidates
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in JD
Hard degree requirement (BS/MS) with no explicit equivalent experience acceptance stated

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years requirement + title)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
compensation.equity_form (partially inferred; JD says 'may be eligible for variable compensation, equity')
ambiguities: Years range stated as '8-10+' — minimum treated as 8 for years_required_min; the '+' implies no upper bound
Degree requirement does not explicitly state 'or equivalent experience' unlike some JDs — treated as hard credential requirement, though equivalent may be accepted in practice
Variable compensation eligibility described as 'certain roles may be eligible' — unclear if this role specifically qualifies
